title: Executive Order 11453—Establishing the Cabinet Committee on Economic Policy

flags.justification: A broad, Cabinet-level committee with purely advisory functions.
Multiple related functions coherently serve one purpose.
A coordination and advisory body with no binding compliance regime.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
A standing coordination body for an ongoing government function, evergreen by nature.

Sec. 4 explicitly states nothing in the order shall subject any agency to another's authority or abrogate any function vested by law — the same protective non-abrogation clause found in EO 11152 and EO 10521.
A coordination body with no third-party compliance regime.
President Nixon's first executive order, establishing his administration's foundational economic-policy coordination mechanism.
Broad, inclusive membership combined with the explicit non-abrogation clause avoids conflict.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 1/12 = 8.3%. Notable as President Nixon's first executive order.
